sections.criterion: Quality and efficiency of the implementation

consistency_log.description: Objective consistency across Excellence, Impact, and Implementation sections, cross-checked against Tier 3 objectives.json
WP reference consistency: all WP IDs referenced in Excellence and Impact exist in the canonical WP list from Implementation (WP1–WP9)

Partner name consistency: all partner references across all three sections use either the exact legal_name or exact short_name from Tier 3 partners.json; no entity suffix drops or variant spellings detected

Deliverable ID consistency: deliverable IDs (D1-02, D2-01, D2-02, D3-01, D3-02, D4-01, D4-02, D5-02, D6-02, D7-02, D8-01, D8-02, D9-01, D9-02) and their titles are consistent wherever they appear across sections

Milestone ID consistency: Implementation references MS1–MS11 (11 milestones); Impact references timing via month numbers (M36, M42, M48) but does not use conflicting milestone IDs; no contradictions detected

KPI consistency and metric completeness: Impact section defines KPI-01, KPI-04, KPI-06, KPI-07, KPI-08, KPI-09, KPI-11 with specific targets; all multi-target objective measurable_targets from Tier 3 (OBJ-1 through OBJ-8) are fully preserved in both Excellence and Impact sections with no partial metric loss

Impact claim grounding: all five impact pathways (PATH-EI-01 to PATH-EI-05) cite project mechanisms (WPs, deliverables, tasks) that exist in the Implementation section's canonical WP/deliverable structure

Budget/resource claim consistency: no specific budget figures, person-month values, or cost allocations are cited in any of the three sections; Implementation notes budget gate passed without numeric values; no conflicting resource claims

Terminology consistency: all canonical component/system names from Tier 3 objective titles are used in full canonical form across all three sections — 'Neuro-symbolic planning engine for autonomous task decomposition', 'Unified adaptive memory architecture', 'Decentralised multi-agent coordination protocols', 'External Tool and API Orchestration Layer', 'Healthcare demonstrator — clinical decision support', 'Manufacturing demonstrator — process optimisation', 'Logistics transfer demonstrator — cross-sector generalisation', 'Open framework release and ecosystem contribution'; no stem-without-keyword occurrences detected

Section ordering and completeness: all three sections present — Excellence (B.1), Impact (B.2), Implementation (B.3) — in correct Part B order confirmed against HE RIA/IA application form template (af_he-ria-ia_en.pdf, page 4: 'Part B includes three sections that each correspond to an evaluation criterion')

Traceability footer consistency: all three sections have non-empty primary_sources arrays; no_unsupported_claims_declaration values are consistent with validation_status.overall_status — Excellence (true/confirmed), Impact (true/inferred), Implementation (true/confirmed); no section has no_unsupported_claims_declaration:true with overall_status:unresolved

Validation status aggregation (informational): Excellence overall_status=confirmed, Impact overall_status=inferred (CLM-07 re EU strategic autonomy contribution is inferred from destination placement), Implementation overall_status=confirmed; no section has overall_status=unresolved

traceability_footer.derivation_note: Assembled draft inherits section-level proposal claim traceability from referenced section artifacts; this artifact adds only section index and cross-section consistency findings.
